build_from.reason: Existing test from Epic 19.6/23.0 already enforces a repo-wide guard that no persona identifier uses the retired role slugs (sentinel, tracer, idiomatic) anywhere in the active persona set (personas/, community personas, index.json). This is the authoritative automated mechanism for AC3.
build_from.suggested_approach: Run the existing test suite (`go test ./personas/... ./internal/personas/...`) as the automated gate for AC3, then conduct a targeted human-readable sweep of docs/, README.md, skill/SKILL.md, and CLI command help strings for any prose mentions the automated test's regex cannot catch.

existing_patterns.name: Retired-slug guard test
existing_patterns.description: personas/retired_slugs_test.go asserts zero references to retired role-based slugs (sentinel, tracer, idiomatic) as persona identifiers across the active persona set. Uses a regex (idiomaticIdentRe) scoped to persona-identifier context.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Automated validation gate for AC3; extend or run as first-line check before prose audit.

existing_patterns.name: Sentinel error / sentinel value (Go idiom)
existing_patterns.description: Distinct from retired persona slug: internal files (internal/verify/severity.go, internal/security/pathguard.go, docs/payload-modes.md, docs/cross-examination.md) use 'sentinel' in the standard Go sense (sentinel error value, sentinel delimiter line). These are NOT stale persona references.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Filter out generic 'sentinel error'/'sentinel line' usages when auditing for AC3; only flag 'sentinel' when used as a persona name.

existing_patterns.name: Sharded technical debt README format
existing_patterns.description: .planning/technical-debt/README.md holds all tracked technical debt sharded by severity, group, and status ([ ] open, [/] deferred, [x] resolved).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Destination for MEDIUM/LOW findings triaged during the codebase review pass per AC1.

existing_patterns.name: Website source of truth formatting
existing_patterns.description: docs/ directory contains 29 self-contained markdown files indexed by docs/README.md, designed to be imported into atcr.dev.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Formatting standards and link resolution for atcr.dev website compatibility.

integration_gaps.gap: Comprehensive multi-agent code review run over production codebase
integration_gaps.resolution: Dogfood atcr's multi-agent reviewer across production directories and triage findings into CRITICAL/HIGH (must fix) and MEDIUM/LOW (technical debt)

integration_gaps.gap: Adversarial security audit for secrets, TODO/FIXME debt, and dead code
integration_gaps.resolution: Conduct manual security pass targeting hardcoded credentials, sensitive artifacts, and leftover TODOs before public release

integration_gaps.gap: Prose documentation audit for legacy persona names and command drift
integration_gaps.affected_component: README.md, docs/, skill/SKILL.md, cmd/atcr/ inline help
integration_gaps.resolution: Audit all user-facing documentation to ensure sasha/penny/ingrid/ian are used consistently and no legacy names (sentinel/tracer/idiomatic) remain

integration_gaps.gap: Website compatibility validation for atcr.dev import
integration_gaps.resolution: Validate all markdown files in docs/ for clean formatting, working relative links, and self-contained structure

reusable_components.name: retired_slugs_test.go regex guard
reusable_components.usage_example: Run go test ./personas/... to verify zero retired persona slugs exist as identifiers

architecture_notes: The code review pass (Phase 1) MUST precede the documentation sweep (Phase 2) so documentation reflects the finalized, hardened codebase.
CRITICAL/HIGH findings from the multi-agent and adversarial review must be fixed in the codebase before launch; MEDIUM/LOW findings must be sharded into .planning/technical-debt/README.md.
Distinguish technical uses of 'sentinel' (Go error sentinels, payload delimiters) and 'idiomatic' (plain English adjective in prompt prose) from stale persona names to prevent false positives.
The docs/ directory contains 29 markdown files that serve as the canonical source of truth for the atcr.dev website build; all paths and links must be relative and self-contained.
